target.name: Sphere Semi
target.founder: Steven Glinert (CEO, multi-time founder; ex-Kinta AI, ex-Pivotus Ventures; Stanford MA Economics), Mitchell Nahmias (CTO, co-founder of Luminous Computing 2018; Princeton PhD photonic integrated circuits; founding researcher in neuromorphic photonics)

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 10 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Sphere Semi is a Seed-stage semiconductor company that has raised $20M to date. The founding team combines deep domain expertise: CEO Steven Glinert brings serial entrepreneurship experience and economics training from Stanford, while CTO Mitchell Nahmias holds a Princeton PhD in photonic integrated circuits and co-founded Luminous Computing. The broader Chips & Semiconductors sector comprises 11 peer companies—3 public (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private entities including Cerebras, SambaNova Systems, PsiQuantum, Etched, and Tenstorrent—though direct comparables at the Seed stage are limited.

Given the scarcity of tightly comparable Seed-stage data points in semiconductor infrastructure, only one valuation method is defensible here. The Stage-Progression approach applies a standard 2.5x multiplier to the $20M total raised proxy, yielding a $50M implied valuation. This reflects typical Seed-to-next-stage expansion dynamics in frontier hardware, absent sufficient peer-median anchors.

The triangulated valuation range is $42M (low) to $57M (high), centered on $50M. This single-method result carries material uncertainty: the absence of three or more same-stage comparable exits or recent private rounds in photonic semiconductors limits corroboration. Key variables include product differentiation relative to the cited peer set, technical validation milestones, and the timeline to Series A capital. The valuation should be revisited once additional market data or company-specific inflection points emerge.
